fact: September 2 is exactly 245 days into the year, which means there are only 120 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime spider season - house spiders are frantically searching for mates and that's why you suddenly see them everywhere indoors!
The sun rises about 2 minutes later each day around September 2, so if you're trying to wake up early, blame the shrinking daylight!
September 2 falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, making it one of the most 'perfectionist' days of the year according to astrology fans.
Monarch butterflies are typically in full migration mode right now, with some flying over 2,000 miles to reach their winter homes in Mexico!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, September 2 marks the beginning of spring - flowers are popping up while the north gets ready for fall!
The autumn equinox is only about 20 days away, meaning day and night are getting closer to being exactly equal length.
Squirrels are in full 'panic mode' around this date in northern climates, frantically burying nuts and seeds for winter - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
September 2 is often prime time for seeing colorful sunsets in many places, thanks to dust and particles in the atmosphere from late summer heat.
Apple trees are typically heavy with fruit right now in temperate regions, and this is when many varieties reach peak sweetness - perfect timing for apple picking season!
Crickets chirp faster or slower based on temperature, and on September 2 you can actually count their chirps to estimate how warm it is outside!
The moon often appears slightly more orange or yellow around this time of year because of how it sits lower in the sky during early autumn evenings.
